new_classes.definition_en: Water that complies with Directive 2009/54/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council on the exploitation and marketing of natural mineral waters, classified under CN heading 2201. Natural mineral water is characterised by its geological origin and its original purity, and must fulfil the requirements of the Directive.
A quality borne by water indicating that it complies with Directive 2009/54/EC on natural mineral waters, distinguishing it from artificial mineral water and other waters in CN heading 2201.

coverage_explanation: The two key distinguishing criteria from the legal text are: (1) the water must be 'natural mineral water' (i.e. of geological origin and original purity), and (2) it must comply with Directive 2009/54/EC. Both are captured by the new class NaturalMineralWater2201 (as a subclass of MineralWaterOrCarbonatedWater2201) and its associated quality NaturalMineralWaterQuality (bearing the Directive-compliance characteristic). The sibling CN code 22011011 (still natural mineral water) vs 22011019 (carbonated/aerated natural mineral water) distinction is based on carbonation status, which could in principle be expressed via the existing CarbonatedQuality class; however, without explicit legal text for 22011011 in scope here, and since the shared note covers both 22011011 and 22011019 jointly, the carbonation discriminator is not formally axiomatised in this call to avoid over-committing. Coverage is reduced to 0.85 to reflect this gap. The reference to Directive 2009/54/EC cannot be expressed as a named individual in the current TBox (no regulatory document individual present), so this is noted but not modelled as a formal restriction.
